rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type contracts in component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when modifying AADL models
Preserve existing component connections and subcomponent hierarchies
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions
Place AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope for the target component or implementation
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, equations, constants, and assignments
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type contracts when concrete bounds are available
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid encoding AADL property associations as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
